Bug / Task Description: Retrieval quality has regressed for documents processed with the fixed-size chunking strategy. Consumers that rely on neighbouring chunks sharing context at their boundaries are reporting that the seams no longer line up: with a configured overlap, consecutive fixed-size chunks are expected to share exactly the configured number of characters where one chunk ends and the next begins, and the chunks together must still cover the entire document with nothing dropped and no run-on. Right now the boundary-sharing is off for this strategy. Diagnose why the configured overlap is not being honoured and correct it so the seam-sharing and full-coverage contract holds across overlap settings (including small strides and zero overlap). Add a hidden behavioural test under tests/unit/ that pins the boundary-sharing contract.
Commit Subject: fix(chunking): honour configured overlap at fixed-size chunk seams
